fullArticle:
He has covered everything from Marvel to the Oscars, and Marvel at the Oscars. He also writes obsessively about the box office, charting the many hits and misses that are released weekly, and how their commercial performance shapes public perception. In his time at Collider, he has also helped drive diversity by writing stories about the multiple Indian film industries, with a goal of introducing audiences to a whole new world of cinema.

A massive upset took place on the domestic Netflix viewership chart recently, with a movie produced on a reported budget of $1.5 million overtaking a franchise tent pole. The small-budget movie was released theatrically five years ago. It received excellent reviews, but didn't make much of an impression at the box office. However, it found an audience on ...
